(3) Add broker rateHistory entries ONLY with a citable source (broker announcement or a Wayback capture of their pricing page) — never fabricate. Schema needs no changes for any of this. Done when: the 2026 spot-check is recorded (values confirmed or corrected+dated) and any added history entries carry citations. PROGRESS (2026-07-06): verification attempted but the sandbox egress policy blocked cmegroup.com/broker pages (WebFetch/curl 403 on all hosts); via search snippets: Schwab/IBKR/Tradovate/Optimus/TradeStation commissions confirmed unchanged, no evidence of a 2026 retail base-fee hike (CME's Apr-2026 changes are member volume-incentive programs), EdgeClear std + AMP tier unverifiable. No data edited. Re-run with direct web access targeting cmegroup.com's 2026-02-01/04-01 PDFs + AMP's 2026 notice. PROGRESS (2026-07-06, direct web access): re-ran with working egress. cmegroup.com PDFs (incl. cme-fee-schedule-2026-02-01.pdf) time out in the fetcher (too large), but convergent evidence says our mid-2026 composites are STILL CURRENT and need no edit: (a) AMP Futures publishes a CME fee-increase notice every year a retail change lands (2023/2024/2025) and has NO 2026 notice — latest is 2025-02-01; (b) TradeStation's current non-member fee mirror still shows 2024-era base exchange fees (ES/NQ $1.38), i.e. no equity/energy/metals move; (c) CME re-dates the schedule PDF every February regardless of changes, so the 2026-02-01 file existing is not a change signal; (d) the 2026-04-01 CME change is member volume-incentive programs, not retail per-contract base fees. Deliberately did NOT reconcile from TradeStation numbers (their CL/MGC use a different exchange+clearing+NFA basis than our composites). NO data edited. Spot-check RECORDED = confirmed unchanged. Item stays open only for optional byte-level confirmation against the authoritative 2026-02-01/04-01 CME PDFs once a fetcher can handle them.

items.title: Backfill econ-calendar CPI/NFP 2021-2023 + GDP 2021, reconcile unverified 2025 rows
items.prompt: Follow-up from F43 (econ-event calendar). The v1 static/data/econ-events.json shipped with gaps because bls.gov and the St. Louis Fed hosts were egress-blocked at build time: (a) CPI & NFP release dates for 2021, 2022, 2023 were OMITTED entirely (no marker on those days), (b) BEA GDP 2021 was omitted (the early-2021 archive page rendered ambiguously and the builder refused to guess), and (c) the 2025 CPI/NFP rows ARE included but UNVERIFIED — 2025 had a ~43-day government shutdown (Oct-mid-Nov) that cancelled the Oct NFP, pushed Sep NFP to 2025-11-20 and released Oct+Nov together 2025-12-16, so those rows need reconciliation against the authoritative BLS archive. When bls.gov (release-schedule archives) and bea.gov are reachable: transcribe the real CPI + NFP release dates for 2021-2023, backfill GDP 2021, and reconcile the whole 2025 CPI/NFP block against the BLS archive — editing the constant lists in scripts/build-econ-events.mjs (do NOT fabricate dates; omit anything you cannot verify), then re-run the generator + node scripts/build-manifest.mjs and confirm scripts/test-econ.mjs stays green. FOMC (2021-2026), GDP (2022-2026) and EIA are already verified — do not touch them. Done when: the omitted years are filled from citable primary-source dates, the 2025 rows are confirmed-or-corrected, the generator header documents the sourcing, and gates (incl. manifest drift) are green.

items.title: Guardrail: accounts hold identity + entitlements ONLY — never trade data
items.prompt: Guardrail from R24. REFINED 2026-07-06 by synced workspaces (F58–F63; design + owner approval in docs/synced-workspaces.md): the server may now hold synced trade/journal/meta records — but ONLY as end-to-end-encrypted ciphertext it cannot decrypt (E2E, zero-knowledge). So the invariant becomes: NO server-READABLE (plaintext) trade, journal, layout, filter, or workspace-name data in D1/KV/R2 — ever. The account system stores identity + entitlements + opaque wrapped keys + ciphertext blobs (R2 SYNC_BUCKET) + a blinded-id change-index (D1); keys live in memory client-side only and the server never sees a symbol/P&L/note/tag/name. Any FURTHER widening of what leaves the browser still needs an explicit owner-approved decision (the A4 seam / a CloudStore ADR is where it lands). Auth security never depends on the fail-open rate limiter (extends S22): the real controls are the WebAuthn ceremony, single-use TTL'd challenges/recovery tokens, hashed session secrets, and Origin checks. Reject any change that writes PLAINTEXT/server-readable user trading data server-side, that sends any plaintext trade field to /functions, or that leans on rateLimited() as the primary protection.

items.title: Symbols dashboard — per-symbol info cards with expandable detail view
items.prompt: From the owner. A new 'Symbols' screen/dashboard showing an info card for every symbol the user has traded (derived from imported data). Each card: the symbol root, full contract name, current price (updates realtime — NOTE: requires a market-data source; the app is local-only with no egress for trade data, so a public quote API needs an ADR-level decision — evaluate free delayed-quote options and flag the privacy/CSP implications before wiring), the exchange it trades on (CME/CBOT/NYMEX/COMEX from exchange-fees.json), which platforms the user traded it on (file provenance via platformOf), the user's total PnL for that symbol, and which contract months were traded rendered in month-letter format (F/G/H/J/K/M/N/Q/U/V/X/Z — build on F40's expiry parse helper). Clicking a card expands a full-horizontal-width detail module with a per-symbol equity/PnL graph and expanded stats (win rate, trade count, avg win/loss, hold time if fills data). Reuse core compute + curveseries; new screen in src/app/screens/ + nav entry. Done when the screen renders cards for every traded root with accurate stats and the expanded view works on all surfaces (demo included, read-only).

items.title: Sync: snapshot/lock the active workspace for the duration of runSync (cross-workspace corruption)
items.prompt: From R1 pass 9 (synced-workspaces audit). `runSync` in src/app/lib/cloudsync.svelte.ts (~248-287) captures a workspaceId + keys, then does multiple awaited push/pull round-trips through the AMBIENT Store — whose active workspace can change via switchWorkspace() (src/lib/core/store.ts setActiveWorkspace ~921, reached from src/app/lib/dashboard.svelte.ts:528) between awaits, because store.ts keeps a single `dbp` DB-handle singleton (~144) that setActiveWorkspace re-points. Scenario: sync for workspace A is in flight; user switches to B; the in-flight pullAndMerge writes A's decrypted records into B's IndexedDB, and the in-flight pushChanges uploads B's local rows under A's captured id+keys — silent bidirectional cross-workspace corruption. Currently untested (the node mock Store never exercises real switch semantics). Fix (pick one): snapshot+lock the active workspace for the duration of a runSync and abort/retry if it changed across an await; OR block switchWorkspace while a sync is in flight; OR thread a workspace-scoped store handle into runSync instead of reaching through the ambient singleton. Done when: switching workspace mid-sync can no longer merge A's records into B's DB or push B under A's id/keys, and a regression test exercises the switch-during-await path.

items.title: Sync: clearing a note/trade-meta to empty must write a tombstone (deletion never syncs, resurrects)
items.prompt: From R1 pass 9 (synced-workspaces audit). src/lib/core/store.ts saveJournal empty branch (406-415, the `else store.delete(date)` at :413) and saveTradeMeta empty branch (486-496) issue a bare store.delete with NO tombstone write — reached when a user clears the last text/tag/shot of a note (dashboard.svelte.ts:553) or empties a trade-meta record (:344). Because no tombstone is emitted, collectChanges reports nothing for the delete, the server keeps the stale row, and the next since=0 reconcile re-upserts it locally — the cleared note/meta comes back. The explicit deleteJournal/deleteTradeMeta paths already tombstone; this is specifically the clear-to-empty path. Fix: route the empty branch through the same tombstoning delete used by deleteJournal/deleteTradeMeta, in the same transaction. Done when: clearing a note/trade-meta to empty emits a tombstone, collectChanges reports the delete, and a since=0 reconcile no longer resurrects it (add a store-level test on fake-indexeddb).

items.title: Sync API: add server-side grantsCloud entitlement gate + per-user storage quota on /api/sync/*
items.prompt: From R1 pass 9 (synced-workspaces audit). functions/api/sync/{push,pull,workspaces,wrapped-ik}.ts (with the shared upsertRecord in functions/_lib/sync.ts:141) gate only on session + ownership + Origin. There is NO grantsCloud/subscriptionForUser entitlement check and NO per-user record/byte quota, so a free-tier session can push unbounded encrypted blobs — a paywall bypass and a storage-DoS / cost vector. Fix: add a server-side grantsCloud (or subscriptionForUser) check on the mutating routes (push, wrapped-ik PUT, workspace create) that returns 402/403 for non-cloud tiers, and enforce a per-user quota (record count + total bytes) in upsertRecord. Done when: a free-tier session is rejected from the mutating sync routes and a per-user quota bounds stored records/bytes, with tests covering both the tier gate and the quota ceiling.

items.title: Sync: purge resurrection — subscribe controller to erase + reset sync cursors on CloudStore.purge()
items.prompt: From R1 pass 9 (synced-workspaces audit). src/app/lib/cloudstore.ts:124-126 clears local data + tombstones and pushes nothing on purge; its comment claims the controller resets cursors on purge, but NO purge / data:erased subscription exists in src/app/lib/cloudsync.svelte.ts (130-142). So the next since=0 reconcile re-downloads every record the user just purged. Fix: subscribe the sync controller to the erase/data:erased event, reset cursorKey/pushedKey on purge, and decide explicitly whether purge should propagate deletes to the server or hard-block sync until re-enabled (document the choice). Done when: after a purge the next reconcile does not re-download the purged records, and the chosen server-propagation/block behavior is implemented + tested.

items.title: Sync: make trade tombstone suppression LWW-consistent with journal/trademeta/meta (convergence)
items.prompt: From R1 pass 9 (synced-workspaces audit). src/lib/core/store.ts:223 suppressedByTombstone returns `!!tomb` — it suppresses an incoming trade whenever ANY tombstone exists — but the merge core resolves the other stores by last-writer-wins (src/app/lib/cloudsync-core.ts:282). Scenario: device A deletes trade T (tomb@100); device B, offline, enriches T (updated@200) and syncs; the server LWW replaces the delete with B's upsert; A pulls it but addTrades suppresses on the tomb@100 → A permanently lacks T while B has it — the two devices never converge. Fix: make trade merges LWW-consistent — `return !!tomb && tomb.updated >= (incoming.updated ?? 0)`. DELIBERATE BEHAVIOR CHANGE to call out and get owner sign-off: this flips the current local 'a delete always wins over a re-import' default to LWW, so a newer re-import can now resurrect a deleted trade. Update the F58 comment at store.ts:218-222 to reflect the decision. Done when: the two-device delete-then-enrich scenario converges (both devices agree on T), the behavior change is owner-approved + documented, and a test covers it.

items.title: Sync: realize the storeFor tier seam — return wrapped CloudStore for 'cloud', select on resolved tier
items.prompt: From R1 pass 9 (synced-workspaces audit). src/lib/core/entitlements.ts:51-55 ignores its argument (`void tier; return Store`), and App.svelte:95-96 hardcodes storeFor('local') then layers CloudStore via wrapStore gated on isStaging. The design intent (storeFor('cloud') → CloudStore) is unrealized, so a cloud-tier PROD user would get no CloudStore at all. Fix: have storeFor return the wrapped CloudStore for 'cloud' (and the local Store for 'local'), and have App.svelte select on the resolved entitlement tier rather than isStaging — a natural part of the CH16 promotion of synced workspaces. Done when: a cloud-tier user on prod gets a CloudStore-wrapped Store via storeFor('cloud'), staging no longer special-cases the wrap, and the demo surface still gets the in-memory DemoStore (non-mutating).

items.title: Sync: wire onunlocked on the Account-screen UnlockModal (stale cloudSync.status after unlock)
items.prompt: From R1 pass 9 (synced-workspaces audit). src/app/screens/Account.svelte:467 renders <UnlockModal> with NO onunlocked handler, whereas src/app/parts/WorkspaceSwitcher.svelte:271-277 wires onunlocked={onSyncUnlocked} + a refresh. Unlocking the vault from the Account screen therefore leaves cloudSync.status stuck at 'locked' until some other event refreshes it. Fix: pass `onunlocked={() => onSyncUnlocked()}` on the Account-screen UnlockModal to match the switcher. Done when: unlocking from the Account screen refreshes cloudSync.status/unlocked exactly as unlocking from the workspace switcher does.

items.title: Sync tests: add a real-Store / real-server round-trip (node mock masked A251/A252/A255)
items.prompt: From R1 pass 9 (synced-workspaces audit). scripts/test-cloudsync.mjs:150-199 uses a hand-mock Store that never exercises delete-on-empty or the real addTrades tombstone/enrich path, and playwright.config.mjs serves static dist/ with no Functions — so the merge core is tested in isolation but real persistence + real endpoint interactions never run. This gap masked the P1s A251/A252 and the P2 A255. Fix: add a node integration test that runs mergeRecords/pullAndMerge against the REAL Store on fake-indexeddb (including the empty-clear delete path and updateTrade enrich), and/or a miniflare-backed e2e that does a genuine push→pull round-trip against the Worker. Done when: a test exercises the real Store's tombstone/enrich/clear-to-empty behavior through a full pull+push cycle and would have caught A251/A252/A255.

items.title: Sync: remove the dead 'Synced workspaces — coming later' card + stale header comment in Account.svelte
items.prompt: From R1 pass 9 (synced-workspaces audit). src/app/screens/Account.svelte:470-482 still renders a legacy 'Synced workspaces — coming later' card (and a stale header comment) even though synced workspaces now exist behind the vault — it contradicts the shipped feature and renders on all surfaces. Fix: remove the dead card and correct/remove the stale comment. Done when: the contradictory card is gone and the Account screen reflects the shipped synced-workspaces state.

items.title: Sync: preserve `updated` for setup/savedFilters meta rows through importAll (re-merge churn)
items.prompt: From R1 pass 9 (synced-workspaces audit). src/lib/core/store.ts:764-765 restores the setup / savedFilters meta records without their `updated` timestamp, so every reconcile treats them as newly changed and re-pushes/re-pulls them. Fix: preserve `updated` through importAll for the meta rows. Done when: a no-op reconcile no longer reports setup/savedFilters as changed.

items.title: Sync API: assign record seq atomically in upsertRecord (concurrent-push seq collision)
items.prompt: From R1 pass 9 (synced-workspaces audit). functions/_lib/sync.ts:101 assigns a record sequence with a non-atomic read-then-write, so two concurrent pushes can collide on a seq. Mitigated today because the client does a since=0 full reconcile on session start, but a long-lived tab syncing incrementally can miss a colliding-seq record until a reload. Fix: assign the sequence atomically — MAX(seq)+1 inside the write, a per-workspace counter row, or a periodic full-reconcile trigger. Done when: two concurrent pushes cannot produce a duplicate/lost seq and an incrementally-syncing tab observes both records without a reload.

items.title: Sync client: type-safety nits batch (method-cast guard, pull PullPage type, dedupe base64UrlToBytes)
items.prompt: From R1 pass 9 (synced-workspaces audit). Three small sync-client fixes: (1) add a runtime guard for the method-string cast at src/app/lib/vault.svelte.ts:133; (2) fix the `pull` return mistyped as `records: []` where it should be PullPage at src/app/lib/cloudsync.svelte.ts:105; (3) dedupe the duplicated base64UrlToBytes at vault.svelte.ts:263-269 by importing the one in src/lib/core/crypto.ts. Done when: all three are addressed, typecheck stays green, and there is a single base64UrlToBytes.

items.title: Sync: isolate per-record decrypt/merge so one poison-pill record cannot wedge the whole pull
items.prompt: From R1 pass 9 (synced-workspaces audit). src/app/lib/cloudsync-core.ts:306-323 decrypts pulled records in a loop with no per-record isolation, so a single corrupt/undecryptable blob throws and aborts the whole pullAndMerge — sync stays broken until that record is gone. Fix: wrap per-record decrypt/merge in try/catch, skip + surface the bad record (count/log), and continue the batch. Done when: an undecryptable record is skipped and the rest of the pull still merges + advances the cursor, with a test injecting a poison-pill record.

items.title: Sync: add passphrase-strength floor and/or raise Argon2id cost (wrapped IK is offline-crackable)
items.prompt: From R1 pass 9 (synced-workspaces audit). functions/api/sync/wrapped-ik.ts GET returns the passphrase-wrapped identity key, and src/lib/core/crypto.ts:50 sets the Argon2id cost — an attacker who obtains the wrapped blob can grind the passphrase offline. Fix: add a client-side passphrase-strength floor (zxcvbn-style minimum) at set-time and/or raise the Argon2id params, and document the residual risk in functions/README.md. Done when: weak passphrases are rejected at set-time and/or the KDF cost is raised, with the residual offline-crack risk documented.

items.title: Sync API: TTL/compaction for webhook_events and tombstones (unbounded D1 growth)
items.prompt: From R1 pass 9 (synced-workspaces audit). schema.sql accumulates webhook_events rows and per-record tombstones forever. Fix: add a TTL / periodic compaction — tombstones can be dropped once every device has reconciled past their seq (or after a safe horizon); webhook_events after the replay window. Done when: a compaction path bounds both tables' growth without breaking dedupe or delete-convergence.

items.title: Sync/billing: clamp dunning grace to first-failure (grace resets on each invoice.payment_failed retry)
items.prompt: From R1 pass 9 (synced-workspaces audit). functions/api/me.ts:49 recomputes the grace deadline off the latest failure, so each Stripe invoice.payment_failed retry restarts the 3-day window and can stretch the effective grace to the full retry span. Fix: clamp the grace to the first-failure timestamp, or document that the retry-span behavior is intended. Done when: the grace window is anchored to the first failure (or the behavior is explicitly documented as intended).

items.title: Sync docs: document CSV-file deletion provenance divergence + functions-only version-bump gap
items.prompt: From R1 pass 9 (synced-workspaces audit). Two documentation items: (1) CSV file records are not synced and fileIds merge union-only, so deleting a CSV on one device leaves stale provenance on another — document the intended behavior in docs/data-flow.md / docs/architecture.md. (2) scripts/bump-version.mjs:97 classifies a functions-only change into neither version track, so a pure-Worker change bumps nothing — flag whether that is desired and document it (in CLAUDE.md / architecture versioning section). Done when: both behaviors are documented (and the version-bump gap is flagged to the owner if undesired).

items.title: Sync: fix stale comments — dead encrypted-name branch in workspaces.ts, 'passkey' vs 'prf' in sync.ts
items.prompt: From R1 pass 9 (synced-workspaces audit). Two comment/dead-branch fixes: (1) the dead encrypted-workspace-name branch at functions/api/sync/workspaces.ts:82-85 — the client never sends `name`, so the branch is unreachable; remove it or note it as reserved. (2) the stale 'passkey' comment at functions/_lib/sync.ts:60 where the actual wire value is `prf`. Done when: the unreachable branch is removed/annotated and the comment matches the real wire value.

items.title: Sync: tombstone store keyed by bare id collides trade vs trademeta (lost trade delete → cross-device duplicate on every edit)
items.prompt: From R1 pass 9 follow-up (synced-workspaces; on the promote path). The `tombstones` object store in src/lib/core/store.ts is keyed by the bare record `id`, but a trade and its per-trade meta share an id — so `updateTrade(oldId,...)` calls deleteTrade(oldId) (writes a `trade` tombstone) then deleteTradeMeta(oldId) (OVERWRITES it with a `trademeta` tombstone). The OLD trade's deletion then never carries a `trade`-typed tombstone, so cloudsync-core.collectChanges never emits a `trade:oldId` delete and the pre-edit trade is never removed on other devices → a duplicate (old + new) after EVERY editor edit — a real cross-device convergence bug. Fix: key the tombstone store by the composite `${type}:${id}` (add a `key` field, keyPath 'key'). The store is new on this unmerged branch, so no prod migration: bump DB_VERSION and, in onupgradeneeded, delete+recreate the tombstones store with the new keyPath (clearing the transient delete-log pre-GA is fine). Update every write path (deleteTrade / deleteJournal / deleteTradeMeta / saveJournal + saveTradeMeta empty-clear / deleteFile cascade / updateTrade's clearTombstone / purge), getTombstones() (hand back the plain {id,type,updated} shape), and the suppressedByTombstone lookup in addTrades (look up `trade:${id}`); mirror all of it in DemoStore. collectChanges already blinds tombstones as `${type}:${id}`, so it just needs getTombstones to yield type+id. Add coverage: a real-Store test that updateTrade on a trade WITH trademeta produces BOTH a `trade` and a `trademeta` tombstone (no collision) and that collectChanges emits the old trade's delete; DemoStore parity. Done when: both tombstones coexist, the old trade delete propagates on reconcile, and typecheck/lint/format/test:unit/build+size all green.

items.title: (discuss + build) Quantower-style scrolling ticker tape in the app header (traded symbols, real-time prices)
items.prompt: Add a Quantower/TradingView-style horizontal scrolling TICKER TAPE to the app header, populated from the DISTINCT roots in the user's imported trades (unique roots from dash.allTrades), each chip showing symbol + last price + change, looping horizontally. ARCHITECT FIRST — the app has no market-data dependency today, so live prices are a NEW external network dependency: assess + document (a) a quote source that actually covers CME FUTURES roots (ES/NQ/CL/GC + micros; continuous-contract mapping; delayed vs realtime; cost/licensing; CORS/browser reachability), (b) transport (websocket stream vs debounced REST poll) + a shared cache + rate budget, (c) client-fetch vs a thin /functions key-hiding proxy. MOAT NOTE: price data is not trade data (no trade field egresses), but it is a new OUTBOUND call — gate it behind a user preference, DEFAULT OFF, so the zero-egress default holds; add the quote host to connect-src in static/_headers (owner-approved, minimal). Deliver the provider/transport assessment (docs/) BEFORE building. Build CSP-clean, no layout shift, pause when the tab is hidden, degrade gracefully when the feed is down (show last-known / hide prices). Staging-gate first. Done when: the assessment is recorded; the ticker renders the user's roots with live-ish prices behind a default-off preference; _headers/CSP updated minimally; offline/feed-down degrades cleanly.

items.title: Module sizing model — Small/Med/Large snap states + carousel grouping (resizable-modules refactor)
items.prompt: The big resizable-modules refactor (expands/supersedes F29 phase-1; the R20 verdict in docs/grid-eval-r20.md was CUSTOM — no gridstack). Give every dashboard module (Dashboard / Analytics / Calendar screens) three SIZE STATES — Small / Medium / Large — each with its own content density/layout, and drive the state from the module's rendered size so a drag-resize SNAPS to the nearest state and stays on the grid. Reference states: the carousel modules (main dash + analytics) = Small; the half-width dashboard modules (trading calendar / break-even / advanced / activity) = Medium; the Calendar-screen calendar + the main-dashboard performance graph = Large. Add a 'carousel' GROUPED state applied when six Small modules sit horizontally in a row. Scope: define the S/M/L contract each module implements (what shows per state), a snap-to-state grid, and corner drag-resize handles that snap to the three states. RESEARCH the build approach first — re-evaluate a UI dependency vs custom against the R20 findings (gridstack / muuri / etc. vs the current keyed-each + styleProps substrate and the A96/A223 bundle budget) and record a short recommendation before the refactor. Constraints: CSP-clean geometry via the styleProps CSSOM action (no inline style=, A55); mobile degrades to a single column; compute() untouched (sizing is layout, not data); persist per-tab layout (extend the modKeyFor payload, migrate the old shape); demo non-mutating; keyboard/a11y resize fallback. Staging-gate. BLOCKS A272. Done when: modules render correctly in S/M/L, resize snaps to the three states + the carousel group, layout persists per tab, mobile degrades, and the build-approach recommendation is recorded.

items.title: (gated by A271) Calendar-screen module dynamically fills the viewport (reduce negative space)
items.prompt: The calendar module on the Calendar dashboard leaves a lot of negative space — make it dynamically size to FILL the available viewport (its Large state) instead of sitting small. GATED BY A271 (the S/M/L module-sizing model): do that first so 'fill the screen' is expressed as this module's Large state within the snap grid, not a one-off hack. Constraints: CSP-clean geometry (styleProps CSSOM action, no inline style=, A55); responsive (degrade to the mobile single-column); no layout shift on load; the day cells stay legible and reflow/scale rather than just stretching. Done when: the Calendar-screen calendar fills the viewport with minimal negative space via its A271 Large state, on desktop and mobile.

items.title: (discuss + build) 'Help' site — Obsidian-style docs/support hub; migrate How-To; add cloud-sync docs
items.prompt: Create a dedicated HELP site (use help.obsidian.md as the reference model — a sidebar-navigated docs hub) and MOVE the existing How-To wiki (src/site/components/Howto.svelte + howto.html) into it. Add: getting-started, the per-platform import guides (from How-To), a support/contact page, and pages documenting CLOUD SYNC (how E2E sync works, the recovery key, subscribing, per-workspace enable, the zero-knowledge guarantee). DISCUSSION FIRST — plan the information architecture (page tree, nav, URL scheme; extend the src/site SSG with a /help section vs a separate surface; keep it prerendered + CSP-clean per A69) and get sign-off on the structure before writing all the content. Update nav/footer + sitemap.xml/robots.txt + _redirects for any moved URLs (howto.html -> /help/...), keeping the drift gate green and preserving old URLs with redirects. Done when: the IA is agreed, the Help hub ships with How-To migrated + support + cloud-sync docs, and every URL move is handled across _redirects/sitemap/canonicals.

items.title: Homepage design refresh — deliberate styling now that we're live (retire the MVP look)
items.prompt: The homepage (src/site/components/Home.svelte) was an early MVP and reads a bit stale now that the product is live. Do a deliberate design refresh — a stronger hero, a clearer value prop + differentiators (local-first / zero-knowledge / futures-focused / the cost+tax model), the pricing tiers now that donations AND the $5/mo cloud-sync subscription are live, platform coverage, and trust/credibility. Keep it CSP-clean (style-src 'self', no inline style=, A55), Tailwind v4 + the greyscale token system (ADR-002), fully responsive, prerendered SSG (A69), and fast (mind the site bundle). Visual/UX pass, no new backend — keep copy consistent with the roadmap/changelog and the live pricing. Walk the UI-mockup workflow (token audit -> mock in code) before committing to the final. Done when: the homepage is redesigned, on-brand, responsive, CSP-clean, and accurately reflects the live product + pricing.

items.title: (assessment) Evaluate migrating to a Tauri desktop shell — cross-platform expansion
items.prompt: Written assessment (docs/, in the style of docs/vault-storage-assessment.md) on wrapping/migrating Blotterbook in a TAURI shell to distribute cross-platform (desktop, maybe mobile) beyond the browser site. Cover: (1) the upside — a real filesystem (unlocks the Phase-3 native VAULT from vault-storage-assessment.md), OS integration, offline packaging, an app-store presence, no browser storage-eviction; (2) pros/cons vs staying a pure PWA/browser app, and vs Capacitor / Electron; (3) implementation difficulty — how the Vite/Svelte SPA embeds in Tauri's webview and what changes (the Store seam / IndexedDB vs native FS, the /functions edge layer vs the same remote API vs a bundled backend, CSP inside a webview, WebAuthn/passkeys in a webview, deep-links for the Stripe return); (4) how dev/build/release/CI change (Rust toolchain, code signing, notarization, auto-update, per-OS builds) and the maintenance cost of a second target; (5) a phased recommendation (browser stays primary; ship a shell only if/when it earns it). The owner is unfamiliar with Tauri — be concrete and decision-oriented. Done when: the assessment is written with a clear recommendation + rough effort.

items.title: (discuss + build) Admin: paginated user table to view/edit per-user entitlements (+ audit the entitlement model)
items.prompt: Add a paginated USER table to the admin panel (src/site/components/Admin.svelte + a new admin /functions endpoint, Cloudflare-Access-gated + noindex like the rest of admin) that lists accounts and lets the owner VIEW and EDIT per-user entitlements — e.g. grant the owner (or a comped user) the CLOUD tier WITHOUT a Stripe subscription. FIRST audit + document how entitlements work today (F60: /api/me derives the tier from the D1 `subscriptions` row via grantsCloud; there is no manual-grant path) and decide the model change: add a manual override (e.g. an `entitlement_override` / `comped` field or a synthetic subscription row) that grantsCloud honors, with an audit trail of who set it and when. Server-side + admin-authed only (NEVER a client-trusted grant — see A277). Paginate the D1 query (never load all users at once). Constraints: admin endpoints behind Access + the admin token (S3/S4), fail-closed without ACCOUNTS_DB, and NO trade data ever surfaced (S25 — identity + entitlement only). Done when: the admin table paginates real users, the owner can grant/revoke the cloud entitlement server-side (reflected by /api/me), the override is audited, and the current-vs-new entitlement model is documented.

items.title: (audit) Verify no auth / entitlement enforcement is trusted client-side
items.prompt: Focused security audit proving that NO authentication or entitlement/paywall enforcement is trusted CLIENT-side — every gate must be independently enforced server-side in /functions. Sweep every protected surface: /api/sync/* (session + Origin + owner-authorization + the A253 cloud-tier gate + quota), /api/me tier derivation, /api/account/* ceremonies + the session-cookie handling, /api/checkout, the admin endpoints (Access JWT + admin token), the staging _middleware key-gate, and the F56 account launch gate. For each, confirm the server re-checks identity/ownership/entitlement and that removing or spoofing the client-side gate cannot escalate access, read another user's data (even ciphertext), bypass the paywall, or reach admin. Explicitly exercise the 'attacker holding a valid FREE-tier session cookie hits the endpoints directly' case, plus unauth + cross-user + wrong-origin. Distinguish client checks that exist only for UX (fine) from ones acting as the security boundary (must ALSO be server-side). Complements R1 pass 9 (which fixed A253's client-only entitlement gap) — go broader. Read-only; deliver a written report (docs/) and file any gaps as new items. Done when: every protected route is confirmed server-enforced (or a gap is filed), with the direct-request attack cases exercised.

items.title: (discuss + build) In-house Stripe subscription form (replace the Checkout redirect) + smooth the get-cloud-sync flow
items.prompt: Replace the Stripe Checkout REDIRECT (/api/checkout -> hosted Checkout) with a HAND-ROLLED in-app subscription form that connects to Stripe via Stripe.js + Elements / Payment Element (PCI-safe — raw card data never touches our code), so subscribing happens inside the app without leaving for Stripe's hosted page. DISCUSSION/PLANNING FIRST — design the flow: loading Stripe.js (CSP additions for js.stripe.com script + the Elements iframe: script-src/connect-src/frame-src — owner-approved, minimal, documented), a /functions endpoint to create the Subscription/SetupIntent + return the client secret, 3DS + error handling, and preserving the account linkage (client_reference_id / subscription metadata, per #120). ALSO smooth the 'Get cloud sync' entry (tabled here): today a logged-OUT user with no local data clicks the homepage CTA -> lands in the app's first-run/onboarding state -> must then navigate to Account -> Subscribe; a logged-in user with data lands on Account directly. Carry the subscribe INTENT through login/onboarding so a fresh user lands on the subscribe step (not the empty dashboard), regardless of session/data state. Keep the moat + fail-closed posture; demo never sees it. Done when: users can subscribe via an in-app Elements form (no hosted-Checkout redirect), 3DS/errors handled, account linkage intact, CSP updated minimally, and the get-cloud-sync flow lands users on the subscribe step smoothly from any state.

howToDrive: One item per prompt; one surface per prompt where possible.
Lead with the *goal*, not the mechanism; let me pick the workable path.
If you batch, name the 1–3 must-haves in case I run low on budget.
Keep using the change-plan + memory pattern for any multi-step item.
